page: Amazon Web Services r5.large is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz. The configuration provides 2 vCPUs across 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, backed by 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate that the r5.large sits in the lower performance tiers for compute-heavy tasks. The single-core stress-ng score of 1275.89 and multi-core score of 1675.39 are categorized as weak and poor respectively, reflecting the low core count. Memory bandwidth tests show 93.25 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 13.27 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, which represents a performance tradeoff where memory capacity is prioritized over raw processing speed and high-throughput memory bandwidth.

This instance is designed for memory-bound workloads that do not require high CPU performance or local storage. It is suitable for small-scale databases, caching layers like Redis, and lightweight enterprise applications that benefit from a high memory-to-vCPU ratio. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5.large offers a low-cost entry point into the memory-optimized category, allowing applications to leverage 16 GB of RAM without paying for unnecessary compute cores.
description: Amazon Web Services r5.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, running on the Nitro hypervisor. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor at 3.1 GHz, this x86_64 instance provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the lower tiers, with a single-core Geekbench score of 1015.0 and a multi-core score of 1205.0, indicating that compute power is limited. Consequently, the r5.large is best suited for memory-intensive but compute-light workloads, such as small database instances, caching layers, and basic web serving. It offers qualitative cost efficiency by providing a high memory allocation without the expense of additional CPU cores.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 16 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5.large is a memory-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, built on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual instance delivering a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for lightweight databases and caching workloads.

16.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Nitro hypervisor architecture with 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for small databases, caching layers, and memory-bound tasks
